1939.example_clue: An SS false flag operation at Gliwice on August 31 of this year was one pretext for an attack on Poland the next day

underground.example_clue: John Legend was an executive producer of this TV series about a group of escaping slaves -- just one word, no "Railroad"

john adams.example_clue: In 2008 Paul Giamatti donned a powdered wig to play this Founding Father; Danny Huston played his cousin Samuel

berlin.example_clue: Tom Tykwer, known for directing "Run Lola Run", returned to the same city to co-create the Weimar Republic drama "Babylon" here

garfield.example_clue: The 2025 limited series "Death by Lightning" is also a game of who's behind the beard; it's Michael Shannon as this president

masters of the air.example_clue: It's been noted that one B-17 mission could last longer than this whole miniseries about the 100th Bomb Group

rasputin.example_clue: With the best of intentions, Czar Nicholas II left healthcare of his only son Alexei to this "Mad Monk"

theodore roosevelt.example_clue: In 1884 he left his infant daughter Alice with his sister while he lived on a ranch in the Dakotas

vlad.example_clue: Aka Dracul, the II prince of Wallachia of this name gave up 2 sons to an Ottoman sultan with little intention of getting them back

sir thomas more.example_clue: This Tudor statesman & saint had his daughters educated; one paid him back by retrieving his severed head for a decent burial

las meninas.example_clue: Spanish king Philip IV & his niece wife let their infanta shine in the center of this Velázquez painting

pickett.example_clue: Confederate Gen. Longstreet was actually in command of the disastrous Gettysburg attack known as this man's "Charge"

vicksburg.example_clue: The Union won at Gettysburg on July 3, 1863; on July 4, Confederates surrendered this vital city in the west

yorktown.example_clue: The site of a key siege during the Revolutionary War, this Virginia port was taken by the Union after another siege in 1862

shiloh.example_clue: This April 1862 Union victory with a biblical name shocked both sides with more than 20,000 total casualties

hampton roads.example_clue: The Monitor & the Merrimack slugged it out in this Virginia harbor with a name that includes a short form of "Roadstead"

brexit.example_clue: In 2016 the vote on this 6-letter choice was over 50% "Leave" in England & Wales, over 55% "Remain" in Scotland & Northern Ireland

the counter-reformation.example_clue: Taking office in 1534, Paul III was the first pope of this movement that pushed back on Protestantism

henry (the navigator).example_clue: This 15th century Portuguese prince became "the Navigator" despite not going on any of the expeditions he sponsored

giuseppe garibaldi.example_clue: This "Hero of Two Worlds" successfully fought for the unification of Italy backed by his volunteer army of about 1,000 men

romania.example_clue: Timisoara in the West was the focal point of protests that brought down this country's Communist leadership in 1989

richmond.example_clue: That turncoat Benedict Arnold had his biggest day in a British uniform when his troops burned this Virginia capital Jan. 5, 1781

earth day.example_clue: It was for the first of these enviro-events that Walt Kelly changed a War of 1812 quote to "We have met the enemy & he is us"

massachusetts.example_clue: In the mid-19th century, most of the shoes & boots made in the U.S. came from this state, from places like Natick & Haverhill

the catholics.example_clue: Amid immigration fears, the American Protective Assoc. was formed in 1887; its oath denounced the "fanaticism" of this religious group

stephen austin.example_clue: In 1834 this Founding Father of Texas was imprisoned in Mexico for almost a year, accused of inciting insurrection

the sphinx.example_clue: Kevin & Karen have thoughts on this 240'-long, 66'-high sculpture; why a lion's body & not our daughter Cassie's?

the rosetta stone.example_clue: Kevin is angry! This 3'9" piece of black granitic stone has a decree from Ptolemy V Epiphanes, but the Patras needed it for their kitchen reno!

barley.example_clue: "I'm telling Cousin Cleo", Karen informs the manager over a spoiled batch of this grain Egyptians loved & Budweiser uses to make beer

the library of alexandria.example_clue: Pals with a board member, the Patras cut the line at the checkout desk of this institution, part of the Shrine of the Muses

memphis.example_clue: "That plinth is too tall!" The Patras are awful HOA members in this city near modern Cairo, once paired as capital with Thebes

the confederate states of america.example_clue: By 1861 11 former U.S. states had seceded to form the new entity with this full formal name

(john) brown.example_clue: A group of wealthy abolitionists known as the Secret Six funneled money to this militant activist

new orleans.example_clue: With a population of about 170,000, this city on the Mississippi was the most populous Southern city at the start of the war

new york city.example_clue: As many as 1,200 may have died in the 5-day draft riots in this city

virginia.example_clue: By 1871, 300,000 Union dead & even some of their foes were buried in natl. cemeteries such as Fredericksburg in this state

babylon.example_clue: The Marduk Gate & the Ishtar Gate were 2 of the 8 fortified entrances into this ancient city

shogun.example_clue: In 1603 Tokugawa Ieyasu was formally appointed to this position by the emperor

(ferdinand) magellan.example_clue: A failed attempt to convert a chieftain to Christianity led to this man's death at the Battle of Mactan in 1521

aristotle.example_clue: A medieval legend said this tutor of Alexander the Great got ridden like a horsey by Phyllis, a consort at the Macedonian court

(toussaint) louverture.example_clue: Before dying in a French jail in 1803, this Caribbean leader craftily played European super powers against one another

(lech) wałęsa.example_clue: In the 1980s the trade union Solidarity led by this electrician challenged Soviet control of Poland

kraków.example_clue: Before he was Pope John Paul II, father Karol Wojtyla had been archbishop of this city of southern Poland for 14 years

brave.example_clue: Boleslaw I, the first king of Poland beginning in 1025, was known by this epithet also beginning with "B"

teutonic (knights).example_clue: 1400's Battle of Grunwald marked a major victory of a Polish Lithuanian army over this order of knights

the alps.example_clue: Of this range prone to avalanches, an Austrian officer in WWI said, "The mountains in winter are more dangerous than the Italians"

afghanistan.example_clue: The U.S. fought its longest war against this country known for harsh winters; in the early years, fighting waned during cold weather

the battle of the bulge.example_clue: Freezing temps & heavy snowfall made for a hard-won Allied victory in this WWII battle, aka the Ardennes Counteroffensive

finland.example_clue: The so-called Winter War with the USSR ended badly for this country in 1940, despite its shrewd use of skiing to move troops

morristown.example_clue: Even worse than Valley Forge, Washington's encampment in this N.J. "town" 1779-1780 was known as "The Hard Winter"

adventure time.example_clue: "Come on, grab your friends, we'll go to very distant lands, with Jake the dog & Finn the human, the fun will never end, it's..."

what we do in the shadows.display: What We Do in the Shadows
what we do in the shadows.example_clue: "Don't sing if you want to live long, they have no use for your song, you're dead, you're dead, you're dead... & out of this world"

ted lasso.example_clue: Mumford (no Sons): "Yeah, might be all that you get, yeah, I guess this might well be it, but heaven knows I've tried"

the wire.example_clue: Bluesy in Baltimore: "You gotta keep the devil way down in the hole"

slow horses.example_clue: A recent Jagger little pill: "Surrounded by losers, misfits & boozers... you made one mistake, you got burned at the stake"

nelson mandela.example_clue: Notable attendees at this ex-world leader's funeral in 2013 included President Obama, Prince Charles, Bono & Oprah Winfrey

measles.example_clue: In 2025 Johns Hopkins spotted that U.S. cases of this disease hit the highest level since it was declared eliminated in 2000

arab spring.example_clue: It's the "seasonal" term for the pro-democracy movements in the Middle East & North Africa in 2010

spirit.example_clue: Sadly, in May 2009 this rover, Opportunity's twin, got stuck in soft Martian sand, & its roving days were over

richard iii.example_clue: Folks paved paradise & put up a parking lot over his bones, but he was reburied in 2015; his coffin bore his name & "1452-1485"

reconstruction.example_clue: In 1967 Robert Clark became the 1st Black member of the Miss. legislature since this immediate postbellum era & served until 2004

world war i.example_clue: During this war's Meuse Argonne offensive, Mexican-born U.S. Private Marcelino Serna singlehandedly captured 24 soldiers

macy's.example_clue: In the 1800s Margaret Getchell suggested that the founder of this dept. store use his red star tattoo as the store's logo

sequoyah.example_clue: With the help of his daughter Ahyoka, he presented his syllabary to Cherokee leaders in 1821

the central pacific.example_clue: Foreman Chin Lin Sou helped recruit for this company that built the Transcontinental Railroad from the western side

everest.example_clue: Did 1920s climber George Mallory, whose body was discovered here in 1999, die going up or coming down from the top?

the ark of the covenant.example_clue: Where is this object, Aron Ha-Berit in Hebrew? & if you say in a government warehouse, we'll ask you to show us

druids.example_clue: Who built Stonehenge way back in the 3000s B.C., millennia before the first record of these learned Celts?

atlantis.example_clue: What exactly is the Yonaguni Monument , discovered in the 1980s & known as the Japanese this place of legend?

missing link.example_clue: Who created the Piltdown Man, a hoax once thought to fill in this hypothetical gap in the chain of human evolution?

aluminum.example_clue: The capstone of the Washington Monument is of this, then more valuable than gold but soon to be mass smelted by Alcoa

rivian.example_clue: The end of an EV tax credit in 2025 hurt sales for Tesla & other makers like this one about to introduce the R2

tennessee.example_clue: Hatch Show Print, in business since 1879, produced the posters when singer Roy Acuff ran for governor of this state in 1948

ibm.example_clue: This co. was born in 1911, after 4 years of talks among the Intl. Time Recording Co., Computing Scale Co. & Tabulating Machine Co.

afro sheen.example_clue: In the 1960s & '70s Black-owned Johnson Products was famous for this item whose name includes a hairstyle

dixie.example_clue: This term for the South of the United States was popularized by a song written in the 1850s

nehru.example_clue: India's longest-serving prime minster, he held office from 1947 until his death in 1964

the opium wars.example_clue: The first of these wars began in 1839 when British warships destroyed a Chinese blockade of the Pearl River at Hong Kong

urban.example_clue: The first of 8 popes of this name reigned from 222 to 230 during the rule of Emperor Severus Alexander

likud.example_clue: This conservative Israeli political party first came to power in 1977 with Menachem Begin as prime minister

the great wall of china.example_clue: The Juyong, Daoma & Zijing Passes of this structure were the 3 strong fortifications close to the Ming capital

reparations.example_clue: Plans for these German post-WWI payments included Owen Young's in 1929 setting them at 121 billion marks

lafayette.example_clue: In 1777 this 19-year-old French aristocrat accepted a commission of major-general for the American cause without pay

santa anna.example_clue: Losing a leg in an 1838 battle didn't stop this general from becoming acting pres. of Mexico in 1839 & dictator soon thereafter

the praetorian guard.example_clue: Pertinax was one of several Roman emperors killed by this guard it did not pay to disgruntle

patrick henry.example_clue: In a famous speech to the House of Burgesses in 1765, he said, "If this be treason, make the most of it"

annan.example_clue: In 2001 the Nobel Peace Prize was jointly awarded to the U.N. & this secretary-general from Ghana

edwin m. stanton.example_clue: This Secretary of War was nominated to the Supreme Court by Ulysses S. Grant but died after being confirmed by the Senate

caligula.example_clue: Suetonius writes that this man gave his horse a marble stall & purple blankets & "it is... said" wanted horsey in high office

biko.example_clue: This founder of South Africa's Black Consciousness Movement died in police detention in 1977

selina meyer.example_clue: Once "Veep", this character becomes prez; also, accused of being Europhobic, she asked if that meant she was scared of subtitles

bartlett.example_clue: On "The West Wing", this president says he can't pardon a Thanksgiving turkey, but drafts the bird into military service to save it

dr. strangelove.example_clue: Bombs away! President Merkin Muffley has an uncomfortable conversation with his "pal" Dmitri in this 1964 film

idiocracy.example_clue: Terry Crews was President Dwayne Elizondo Mountain Dew Herbert Camacho in this 2006 comedy where smarts were in short supply

dave.example_clue: In this 1993 film president Kevin Kline brings in CPA Charles Grodin to "help me cut the budget a little"

the trojan horse.example_clue: Laocoön warned his fellow citizens, but they let this huge wooden animal inside their gates anyway

pompey (the great).example_clue: Losing the high ground, he moved his troops onto the plain at the Battle of Pharsalus to meet Caesar -- not so "great", man!

an eclipse.example_clue: In tradition, Hsi & Ho had the job of predicting this, so China could arm against the sun-eating dragon, but got drunk instead

cicero.example_clue: This dude could not. Stop. Talking! Octavian "should be given praise, distinctions -- & then be disposed of"; ooooops

pyrrhus.example_clue: Some advice for this king of Epirus, master of the hollow victory; if a tile is thrown at you from a roof in Argos, duck! Or die!

fahrenheit 451.example_clue: Part 3 of this Ray Bradbury classic is titled "Burning Bright"

ragtime.example_clue: After this most famous novel, E.L. Doctorow moved up in time a few decades to the Depression in "Loon Lake"

little house on the prairie.example_clue: The best known in a series of books, it came between "Farmer Boy" & "On the Banks of Plum Creek" in order of publication

the joy luck club.example_clue: Amy Tan dedicated this 1989 bestseller "to my mother and the memory of her mother"

the executioner's song.example_clue: Norman Mailer chronicled the life, crimes & death of convicted killer Gary Gilmore in this 1980 Pulitzer Prize winner

the civil rights act.example_clue: This 1964 act outlawed discrimination based on race, color, religion or national origin

the articles of confederation.example_clue: Enacted in 1781, this first charter of U.S. govt. failed to give Congress the power to tax, leaving it largely ineffective

the spanish-american war.example_clue: The Battle of San Juan Hill was the bloodiest for U.S. forces in this war; our foe had half as many casualties

eleanor roosevelt.example_clue: This former first lady had a major role in drafting the United Nations' Universal Declaration of Human Rights

muckraking.example_clue: Ida Tarbell 's reporting on Standard Oil was part of the early 1900s journalism derisively called this movement

fdr.example_clue: In April 1945 The New York Times reported his last words: "I have a terrific headache"

dot-com.example_clue: Fueled by the Internet, the rapid rise in tech stocks in the late '90s known as this bubble burst big time in 2000

go west, young man.example_clue: This famous piece of advice attributed to Horace Greeley continues, "and grow up with the country"

the union pacific.example_clue: Right on track, in 1869 this company's railroad, extending from Omaha, met up with another at Promontory, Utah

the free soil party.example_clue: Here's the dirt -- active from 1848 to 1854, this third party opposed the expansion of slavery in new territories

the tudor dynasty.example_clue: This ruling dynasty of English monarchs that came to power in 1485 originated in Wales

the philippines.example_clue: In 1521 Magellan claimed these Asian islands for Spain, which held them until losing a war in 1898

the barbary coast.example_clue: 17th century Muslim pirates who gave 10% to their wealthy backers operated off this North African coast named for a local people

iran.example_clue: Rivals of Rome, the Parthians ruled a vast empire from their kingdom south of the Caspian Sea in what is today this nation

the seneca.example_clue: This Native American people who share their name with one of the Finger Lakes were the largest in the Iroquois Confederacy

the o.k. corral.example_clue: Biographer Stuart N. Lake called the shootout at this place "the key to the eminence of Wyatt Earp"

gettysburg.example_clue: Cemetery Hill was the main site of the Union Army's artillery at this Civil War battle in July 1863

avignon.example_clue: Seven popes would reign from this city on the Rhône River after Pope Clement V moved the seat of the papacy there in 1309

the boxer rebellion.example_clue: In 1900 churches & homes of foreign-born people in Beijing were burned during this rebellion

tenochtitlan.example_clue: On an island in the middle of Lake Texcoco, the place then known as this was captured by the Tlaxcaltec & the Spanish in 1521

an amoeba.example_clue: The smallest host of the bacterium that causes leprosy is this one-celled organism with a name that means "change" in Greek

south africa.example_clue: This country's Robben Island once housed a leprosy settlement & an infamous site for political prisoners

ben-hur.example_clue: In this work by Lew Wallace, Jesus cures the mother & sister of the title character of leprosy

hansen.example_clue: The causative agent of leprosy was found by Albert Neisser & this Norwegian man the disease was later named for

(father) damien.example_clue: He was canonized more than 100 years after he volunteered to minister to patients with leprosy on the island of Molokai
